PlayerAccount.title: PlayerAccount
PlayerAccount.description: Represents a player's core profile, linked to an external authentication system, and stores game-specific account data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerAccount entity.

email.description: Player's email address, primarily for communication or account recovery (not for authentication).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the player account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the player's last successful login.

currencyBalance.description: The total amount of in-game currency the player possesses.

agentIds.description: References to Agent entities owned by this player. (Relationship: PlayerAccount 1:N Agent)

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quest entities currently active or assigned to this player. (Relationship: PlayerAccount N:N Quest)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an in-game character or entity (player-controlled or NPC-controlled) that performs actions like gathering, combat, or exploration within the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

ownerPlayerId.description: Reference to the PlayerAccount entity that owns this agent, if player-controlled. (Relationship: PlayerAccount 1:N Agent)

ownerNpcId.description: Reference to the NPC entity that controls this agent, if NPC-controlled. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Agent)

currentLocationX.description: The X-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentLocationY.description: The Y-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

currentLocationZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the agent's current position in the game world.

status.description: The current activity status of the agent, e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'combat', 'trading', 'exploring'.

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

stamina.description: The current stamina points of the agent.

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item entities currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Defines Non-Player Characters with unique personalities, often generated by AI, inhabiting the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

personalityDescription.description: A descriptive text outlining the NPC's personality and characteristics, dynamically generated by AI.

agentId.description: Reference to the Agent entity that represents this NPC in the game world. (Relationship: NPC 1:1 Agent)

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents an in-game objective or mission that players or agents can undertake, often dynamically generated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest, including its backstory and objectives, potentially generated by AI.

objectives.description: A list of individual objectives that must be completed to finish the quest.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities awarded upon quest completion. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

rewardCurrencyAmount.description: The amount of in-game currency awarded upon quest completion.

status.description: The current status of the quest, e.g., 'available', 'active', 'completed', 'failed'.

assignedPlayerId.description: Reference to the PlayerAccount entity to whom this quest is assigned. (Relationship: PlayerAccount 1:N Quest)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents any in-game object, resource, or currency, whether found as loot, crafted, or purchased from a storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its properties.

type.description: The category of the item, e.g., 'Weapon', 'Armor', 'Resource', 'Consumable', 'Currency', 'QuestItem'.

iconAssetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset entity for the item's icon. (Relationship: Item 1:N GameAsset)

modelAssetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset entity for the item's 3D model, if applicable. (Relationship: Item 1:N GameAsset)

stackable.description: Indicates if the item can be stacked in inventory.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of this item that can be stacked in a single inventory slot, if stackable.

value.description: The base monetary value of the item in in-game currency.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item, e.g., 'Common', 'Uncommon', 'Rare', 'Epic', 'Legendary'.

effectDescription.description: A description of any special effects or benefits the item provides.

storePrice.description: The price of the item when purchased from the secure Axiom Storefront. Null or 0 if not sold in store.

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Manages references to digital assets (textures, models, audio) stored in Firebase Storage, used for dynamic loading in the Godot client.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: A human-readable name for the asset.

type.description: The classification of the asset, e.g., 'Texture', 'Model', 'Audio', 'Icon', 'Animation'.

storagePath.description: The Firebase Storage path (URI) where the asset file is located.

mimeType.description: The MIME type of the asset file, e.g., 'image/png', 'model/gltf+json'.

version.description: A version identifier for the asset, useful for cache busting and updates.

checksum.description: A checksum (e.g., MD5 or SHA256 hash) for verifying asset integrity after download.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records details of purchases made by players through the secure Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerAccount entity who made the purchase. (Relationship: PlayerAccount 1:N Transaction)

purchasedItemIds.description: References to Item entities purchased in this transaction. (Relationship: Transaction N:N Item)

purchasedQuantities.description: The quantity of each corresponding item purchased. Array length must match `purchasedItemIds`.

amountPaid.description: The total monetary amount paid for the transaction.

currencyType.description: The real-world currency used for the transaction, e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'.

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the transaction was completed.

paymentGateway.description: The payment gateway used for the transaction, e.g., 'PayPal'.

status.description: The current status of the transaction, e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded'.

externalReceiptId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the external payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Stores global, persistent game world data and dynamically calculated metrics like civilization index and economy parameters. This entity could represent a singleton or specific regions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity. Often a singleton 'global' ID or region-specific.

civilizationIndex.description: The current overall civilization index of the game world, reflecting its development and complexity.

economyInflationRate.description: The current inflation rate affecting the in-game economy.

lastHeartbeatRun.description: Timestamp of the last successful 'world heartbeat' execution.

currentSeason.description: The current in-game season or cycle.

weatherState.description: A description of the current global weather conditions in the game world.

GameParameter.title: GameParameter
GameParameter.description: Defines administrative-configurable parameters for the game world, allowing for dynamic adjustments to game mechanics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameParameter entity, typically a programmatic key like 'loot_drop_rate'.

key.description: A unique, descriptive key identifying the game parameter.

value.description: The current value of the parameter, stored as a string for flexibility and parsed by the application.

dataType.description: The expected data type of the parameter's value, e.g., 'number', 'boolean', 'string', to aid client-side parsing.

description.description: A clear explanation of what this game parameter controls.

lastModifiedAt.description: Timestamp when this parameter was last updated.

modifiedByUserId.description: Reference to the PlayerAccount ID (or admin user ID) who last modified this parameter. (Relationship: PlayerAccount 1:N GameParameter)

definition.description: Stores a player's core account profile and game-specific data. Documents are directly owned by the authenticated user (UID matching playerId).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player, corresponding to request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Stores agent entities that are directly owned and controlled by a specific player. Includes denormalized 'ownerPlayerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player who owns this agent.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique ID of the agent.

definition.description: Stores quest entities that are currently assigned to or active for a specific player. Includes denormalized 'assignedPlayerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player to whom this quest is assigned.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique ID of the quest.

definition.description: Records purchase transactions made by a specific player through the Axiom Storefront. Includes denormalized 'playerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player who made the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Defines global Non-Player Character entities with unique personalities. Read-only for players, writable by admins/Cloud Functions.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique ID of the NPC.

definition.description: Stores agent entities that are controlled by NPCs, distinct from player-owned agents. Read-only for players, writable by admins/Cloud Functions.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique ID of the NPC-controlled agent.

definition.description: The global master catalog of all in-game items, resources, and currency definitions. Globally readable for all clients, writable by admins/Cloud Functions.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique ID of the item.

definition.description: Manages references to digital assets (textures, models, audio) stored in Firebase Storage. Globally readable, writable by admins/Cloud Functions.

params.name: assetId
params.description: The unique ID of the game asset.

definition.description: Stores global, persistent game world data and dynamically calculated metrics. Typically a singleton ('global') or regional documents. Globally readable, writable by Cloud Functions/admins.

params.name: worldStateId
params.description: The unique ID of the world state entity (e.g., 'global').

definition.description: Defines administrative-configurable parameters for game mechanics. Globally readable for game client configuration, writable by admins. Includes 'modifiedByUserId' for audit purposes.

params.name: parameterId
params.description: The unique ID (key) of the game parameter.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at this path signifies that the corresponding userId is an administrator. This allows for simple, performant security rules based on document existence without custom claims.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who holds an admin role, corresponding to request.auth.uid.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for 'Axiom Frontier' is designed to uphold stringent security, scalability, and debuggability principles. Core to this design is **Authorization Independence**, achieved primarily through a combination of path-based ownership and explicit denormalization of authorization context within documents.

For all player-specific data such as `PlayerAccount` itself, `Agent` (player-controlled), `Quest` (player-assigned), and `Transaction` records, a strict hierarchical path `/playerAccounts/{playerId}/<subcollection>/<docId>` is employed. This directly embeds the `playerId` (which maps to `request.auth.uid`) into the document path, making authorization rules incredibly simple and atomic: a user's access to a document is determined solely by matching `request.auth.uid` with the `{playerId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates any need for expensive and complex `get()` calls to parent documents within security rules.

Further reinforcing Authorization Independence, key ownership fields like `ownerPlayerId` in `Agent`, `assignedPlayerId` in `Quest`, and `playerId` in `Transaction` are explicitly denormalized into the respective documents. While the hierarchical path already ensures this for subcollections, this explicit denormalization guarantees that each document carries its complete authorization context, making rules more robust and simplifying scenarios where documents might be queried or moved. This strategy significantly minimizes dependencies and facilitates atomic operations.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** are directly supported by this structural segregation. For example:
1.  **Private User Data:** `/playerAccounts/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` ensures that a `list` operation by a player on `/playerAccounts/$(request.auth.uid)/agents` will *only* return their own agents, as the path itself acts as a pre-filter. There's no need for a rule to filter results *after* a broader `list` query.
2.  **Public/Global Data:** Collections like `/items`, `/gameAssets`, `/npcs`, `/worldState`, and `/gameParameters` are designed with a homogeneous security posture: they are generally readable by all players (for game state and catalog information) but strictly writable only by Cloud Functions or administrators. This allows clients to `list` these collections efficiently without security rules needing to filter sensitive data.
3.  **Admin Roles:** A dedicated `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection uses existence-based authorization (`get(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)).exists()`), which is highly efficient and aligns with QAPs, as it's not filtering data but checking a direct permission flag.

**Structural Segregation** is applied to distinguish player-owned agents from NPC-controlled agents. Player-owned agents reside under `/playerAccounts/{playerId}/agents`, while NPC-controlled agents are in a separate top-level collection `/npcAgents/{agentId}`. This separation simplifies rules for each agent type, preventing mixed security concerns within a single collection.

**Access Modeling** follows the prescribed patterns: path-based ownership for private player data (`/playerAccounts/{playerId}/...`) and dedicated top-level collections for global, public, or admin-controlled entities. The `modifiedByUserId` field in `GameParameter` serves as an audit trail rather than a primary authorization mechanism, as admin roles are handled via DBAC (`/roles_admin`). Overall, the design prioritizes explicit ownership, minimal rule complexity, and efficient query capabilities without compromising security.
